index.html.title: index
index.html.description:
This site contains my campaign notes for Europa Universalis 4. I am documenting some strategies and tactics that I use.
TBL - The Bohemian Lowlands

campaigns/tbl-1467-stealing-iberia.html.title: TBL 1467 - Stealing Iberia
campaigns/tbl-1467-stealing-iberia.html.description:
The year is 1467. The game looks promising with many possible directions for us, but there will be some RNG involved and we are not very strong ourselves so we will need to wait for the right time to strike.
Aside from obtaining the Personal Union over Hungary using the Bohemia Mission The Hungarian Succession, we are actively hunting for more PUs. Savoy was looking promising but they recently got an heir, and since we don't have Diplomatic Ideas yet we are stuck with the extra relation.
We randomly got a PU over Wolgast, with may prove to be a burden later occupying a precious relation slot. For now I'm keeping them around since they may be useful to feed provinces in the Scandinavian, Baltic or Russian regions.
Sweden does not have an heir, but at 25 years old their ruler is still likely to get an heir, so I do not expect to get my dynasty on their throne at the moment.
Aragon is 15 years old and without an heir, and since we are Trastamara Dynasty we can use Claim Throne and force the union. Forcing a union over the Senior Partner will transfer Junior Partners as well, and Castille is the Junior Partner under Aragon. So is Navarra, but I will ditch them, perhaps as part of the peace deal, or just by abandoning the union after the war.
Burgundy will aid us, and England too if we curry favours with them.
The war is long and exhaustion ticks up to 15. Sieges take forever, and multiple times we get caught on a hostile fort which means we take bad hits and sometimes even lose the entire siege. But we succeed, and now we have our first major PUs outside of the mission tree.
TBL 1475 - Ex-Duke Charles

campaigns/tbl-1475-ex-duke-charles.html.title: TBL 1475 - Ex-Duke Charles
campaigns/tbl-1475-ex-duke-charles.html.description:
It's 1475, and Duke Charles of Burgundy just left us his entire country, for which we are eternally grateful.
None of the PUs (except Wolgast) are currently loyal, so we need to work on that before hostile countries start supporting their independence. This will require building up our armies, which is tough without manpower or any economy. Developing our Gold Mine would be nice, but this requires our most precious Diplomatic Monarch power so I prefer other means.
The Austrian Emperor is now 60 years old and I would love to see a re-election as soon as possible. I still don't have any cardinals so our Papal Influence can't be used yet to aim for becoming the Curia Controller.
As part of the mechanics around the BI, Austria will likely declare war on us soon, so this is a good time to perhaps pick up some extra allies (although it looks like we will be able to beat him easily). Hopefully Austrias attacks will mostly aim at my PUs, reducing their strength a little bit so that they can become loyal sooner.
Still terrible, but that is within expectations. The majority of our wealth is taken from winning wars. Hopefully we can take the Gold Mine from Serbia's Kosovo in a few years, but we do not currently have a CB yet. I've marked a northern province of them as a vital interest, hoping that Hungary will fabricate a claim for me.
TBL 1488 - Mary Falls

campaigns/tbl-1499-project-poland.html.title: TBL 1499 - Project Poland
campaigns/tbl-1499-project-poland.html.description:
It's now 1499 and the Age of Reformation is approaching, changing the game in many ways. The religious game will start, the Dutch may revolt, and we will have to start expanding to the east and south.
I've just used the Polish Diplomacy mission to PU Poland and Lithuania. The war turned out to be very easy. Poland and Lithuania marched into my war ally Sweden to occupy them, leaving their country open for the taking, and I could reach 60% war score using only sieging.
During peacetime, we start annexing Styria and enable the Noble Integration Policy privilege for an additional 5% discount.
The Ottomans have a strong game, but I had feared that they would be a lot stronger by now. Completing the Bohemian Commonwealth mission requires kicking the Ottomans completely out of the Balkans region, so I better get started soon. Poland and Lithuania are loyal yet still have their troops at pre-union levels, so perhaps the right time to strike is now.

I've matched their Discipline by hiring an advisor, and was surprised that the Morale modifiers we picked up along the way is enough to outclass the Ottomans. Since the Ottomans are the #2 Great Power I decide to activate my Golden Age to boost Morale further and make fights more likely to end up in my favour.
I take Improved War Taxes as an Age Bonus since I suspect that this war will take some time and I don't want to bleed money too heavily. Their Quantity Ideas means that we will have to cut through a lot of troops, but hopefully by striking now we can make the Ottomans collapse before they grow too strong to deal with.
TBL 1503 - The First Crusade

mechanics/personal-union.html.title: Personal Union
mechanics/personal-union.html.description: A subject type tied to a ruler. Only applicable for Christian nations. If the senior partner dies, the junior partner can break free under certain conditions.If a nation has a young ruler (e.g. 15 years old) and they have an heir, it is more likely that their ruler dies when their heir is old, meaning that they are a long-term target for a PU.Instead of getting your dynasty on another country's throne, it is sometimes easier to get their dynasty on your own throne. You can then PU them easier. Very useful to switch to Trastamara Dynasty for example, since that makes Castille, Aragon and Naples suitable targets.If you are in a Force Union war, you can change to a different dynasty and still pick the Force Union peace deal option.

tactics/birding.html.title: Birding
tactics/birding.html.description: Letting the game crash on purpose and reloading it. This allows to avoid bad luck or to re-roll events until results are favourable.

tactics/perma-curia-strat.html.title: Perma Curia Strat
tactics/perma-curia-strat.html.description:
Strategy to become Curia Controller and permanently keep it.
Sourced from Lambdaxx's video. Must:
Have a non-Catholic Subject
Have one or more Cardinals
Have 5 or more Papal Influence
Papal States must have a Coastal Province
We use Terracina in this guide Nice
The Pope is very old
The Papal States is small
Become Curia Controller
Declare war on the Papal States
Fully occupy, transfer Terracina occupation to your non-Catholic Subject
Wait until the Pope dies Pause time
Invest 5 PI for Curia Controller Peace out with Full Annexation
Feed all provinces except Rome to your non-Catholic Subject
Accept Rome's Papal Rebel Demands, re-creating the Papal States
This results in you becoming Curia Controller
Creating non-Catholic Papal States
Convert Terracina for your subject to it's religion
Wait for Truce with Papal States to expire
Seize Terracina
Accept Terracina's Papal Rebel Demands
Declare war on the Papal States and fully Annex them
Feed Terracina to your subject
Accept Rome's Papal Rebel Demands
The crucial step is to create a Papal States that is not Catholic. Many traditional ways of (re)creating non-Catholic countries do not work for the Papal States, but it seems that accepting Rebel Demands still allow the Pope to have other religions.
The religion of a released country depends in some way on the religion of the most recently gained province. Therefore, the strategy is to feed the Papal States a non-Catholic province, then re-spawning them using Rebels.It is likely that development also plays a role, but it is not clear to which extend exactly.
Using Birding, the war can be kept short by reloading if the old Pope does not die.

campaigns/tbl-1503-the-first-crusade.htm.title: TBL 1503 - The First Crusade
campaigns/tbl-1503-the-first-crusade.htm.description:
The war against the Ottomans is going quite well, we are looking at achieving a solid victory. It has costed us, but it will set up expansion into the east later, so it is definitely worth it.
The first phase of the war is to somewhat reduce their troop quantity by picking a few favourable fights, and perhaps Stackwipe a few of their squads.
Some of my allies focused on Tunis, which allowed me to peace them out after two years. This turned out to be a mistake since Tunis was the Crusade target, so our side lost useful combat modifiers when peacing them out early. But I did not replay this mistake since my allies would now be free to help in the Balkans.By playing patiently we are able to lure the enemy into defensive fights, which turns it into our favour and helps us to reduce their military influence in the area.

Now that their numbers have decreased enough, and my allies have moved from Tunis into the Balkans, we are able to siege a few forts, including their capital Constantinople. Their siege ability is a lot better so they sometimes take one fort back. We can threaten to collapse on their siege with out superior quantity which causes them usually to abandon the siege, which means they have taken attrition without benefit.So far we are 500 ducats in debt, which is a lot less than I expected.We will have no way to cross into Anatolia because the massive Ottoman fleet is guarding the strait crossing. We will have to wait for them to leave the strait, perhaps we can bait them by sending a few ships nearby. The alternative is to send our troops around, but this would allow the Ottomans to take back their capital.
We want to be able to have good ways to take more Ottoman land in the future, so want to take provinces that allow us to release Bulgaria and Byzantium to use their Reconquest CBs. We take their province, max money and war reparations to cripple their economy as much as possible.

Now would be a great time to set up the Perma Curia Strat since the Pope is 80 years old. We ally Florence to feed them most of the land that we don't need to avoid AE. We have released Bulgaria which is a non-Catholic subject that we will use to execute the strategy. But... unfortunately we have a regency council, so we cannot actually declare the war! Five years of non-war remain. Maybe some time to focus on setting up the next few plays. And maybe it will not be calm years, because...
Since 1500 has passed, the Dutch Revolts disaster has started ticking up. By boosting our stability to +2 and accepting both [[Flemish] and Dutch Culture, we were able to stop the disaster from progressing. However, after a few years some provinces turned Protestant which again gives the disaster progress without us being able to convert them back, meaning that the disaster will fire.We end up with 50K separatists rebels which were not too hard to beat. Every now and them we may have rebels for the next 20 years. I expect that the rebels will be weak enough so that we do not have to release Dutch countries.
TBL 1512 - Europe and Economy

tactics/zero-rebels.html.title: Zero Rebels
tactics/zero-rebels.html.description:
Stack Unrest Reduction until -17 Tolerance Legitimacy
Stack CCR until >= 75% Reform Regional Representation This allows infinite overextension as long as you immediately core the conquered provinces, since rebels cannot tick to 100% faster than your coring time (9 months)

ideas-or-to-do.html.title: Ideas or To Do
ideas-or-to-do.html.description:
Perma Curia Strat from TheStudent
PU hunting

campaigns/tbl-1512-europe-and-economy.ht.title: TBL 1512 - Europe and Economy
campaigns/tbl-1512-europe-and-economy.ht.description:
Now that we can't do much in the east, we make some plays in Europe. Vladimir is on the throne, but our new heir is quite bad so we disinherit him, hoping for a better one. This may also allow us to get a different dynasty, perhaps a Russian one.
After releasing Bulgaria, we ally Florence and call him into a war with the Papal States. The war goes smoothly but unfortunately Florence occupies Roma without desiring it, meaning that we cannot fully annex the Pope. So we make a slightly modified plan for Perma Curia Strat, perhaps adding an additional war with them. But for now we can hand off most Papal land to Florence. We want to grant Terracina to Bulgaria but they do not have enough coring range, so we take it for ourselves and need to hand it off later to a different subject.
A small upside is that Naples got Excommunicated, meaning that Terracina gives us the ability to take provinces from Naples at 50% AE. Austria is allied to Naples which is unfortunate since we would like a separate Tirol Reconquest war later, but since my subject count is already very high we postpone that until later.
Florence is allied to Naples, so we drag him into a war with Serbia first for their Kosovo Gold Mine. This way, we don't have to fight him, instead we can keep our Alliance.
Both wars are very easy, granting us a quick cash boost and the desired land. Serbia and Bosnia are full annexed, while I take four provinces from from Naples.
I grant Napoli to Byzantium (which I released as a subject just before the wars) so that I can give him Terracina too.
Our missions gave us a Subjugation CB on Brandenburg, which expires in one year. Subjugating him would give a lot of AE so I'm not sure whether to actually take them. Perhaps we ally his neighbours and give away as much of his land as possible, giving us less AE and we can Reconquest it later.

campaigns/tbl-the-bohemian-lowlands.html.title: TBL - The Bohemian Lowlands
campaigns/tbl-the-bohemian-lowlands.html.description:
Dutch True One-Culture starting as Bohemia on Very Hard difficulty. I'll be using the tag #tbl for campaign updates.
TBL 1467 - Stealing Iberia
TBL 1475 - Ex-Duke Charles
TBL 1499 - Project Poland
I plan to use a diplomatic style with lots of subjects. Personal Unions early, using the Bohemian Mission Tree for Hungary, Poland and Lithuania and hunting aggressively for additional free unions. Depending on lucky free unions, we can form nations to get missions to PU other nations. A Burgundian PU (or even direct inheritance) is very likely to succeed as well.
I'll be using Birding for planned RNG-based events that will impact the game in big ways large failures in wars I'll indicate when I have done so. So far I'm thinking of picking this order of idea groups:
Diplomatic Ideas
Quality Ideas
Religious Ideas
Influence Ideas
Administrative Ideas ? ? ?

campaigns/tbl-1488-mary-falls.html.title: TBL 1488 - Mary Falls
campaigns/tbl-1488-mary-falls.html.description:
It is now 1488, and the map looks a bit more golden than before. Mary of Burgundy fell off her horse, granting us full ownership of all of her land!
Our PUs are a lot more loyal now that our own development got so much higher.
Now that Burgundy is no longer occupying a relation slot, I attack Saxony to subjugate them via our mission tree. The war is small and easily won, and afterwards I turn them into a PU which means we could get lucky and get a free annexation later on.
The AE is quite bad and I postpone taking Brandenburg. Our war exhaustion is still high, and I decide to take Quality Ideas instead of Religious Ideas in our second slot. Combined with Diplomatic Ideas this gives a policy that reduces War Exhaustion. It may also prove helpful on fighting the Ottomans given that this is very hard difficulty. Not sure if I am going to regret not picking Religious.
In the south I see Algiers which may be a suitable non-Catholic subject later on. In two years, we will fight Austria with Reconquest CB, restoring all cores of Styria, and taking a single province containing a core of Tirol.
Poland took the union over Lithuania, which means our mission to PU Poland will grant us Lithuania as well. Their numbers are high but hopefully we can win by calling in some allies.
TBL 1499 - Project Poland

tags/austria.html.title: Austria
tags/austria.html.description:
Formable Tag, German Regional Tag
  Traditions:+10% Morale of armies+30% Improve relations
 Imperial Ambition+10% Imperial authority growth modifier−15% Diplomatic annexation cost
 Military Frontier−10% Fort maintenance+15% National garrison growth
 Fugger Banking+0.05 Yearly inflation reduction−0.5 Interest per annum
 Habsburg Dominance+2 Diplomatic reputation
 Edict of Restitution+2% Missionary strength
 Austrian Court+1 Diplomatic relation
 Hofkriegsrat+30% Reinforce speed+5% Discipline
 Ambition:+50% Chance of new heirSelection of interesting ones:
Austriae Est Imperare Orbi Universo:
 +2 Diplomatic reputation
 −10% Diplomatic annexation cost
Unlocks the conversion of  Vassals and  Personal Unions into their Incorporated variants
Bulwark of Catholicism:
 +2% Missionary strength
 +1 Tolerance of the true faith
 +2 Yearly Papal Influence
 +0.05 Imperial authority modifier (added after completing the Emperors of the East mission)
Hegemons of Western Europe:
 −10 Years for Personal Union integration
 +25% Chance to Inherit Personal Union Subjects

tags/siam.html.title: Siam
tags/siam.html.description:
Formable Tag that can be useful for One Culture runs. They require to have completed two disasters, so consider Court and Country and
is in the  Tai culture group owns its core provinces: Ayutthaya (600) Sukhothai (601) Chiang Mai (589) owns at least 20 core provinces in Indochina region has completed 2 non-Estate disasters this campaign
  Traditions:+10% Morale of armies+25% National manpower modifier
 Royal Poets+0.5 yearly prestige−15% Administrative advisor cost
 Advanced Elephant Warfare+15% Cavalry combat ability+1 Cavalry fire
 Experienced Ambassadors+2 Diplomatic reputation

+5% Discipline

tags/styria.html.title: Styria
tags/styria.html.description: Hidden tag in Austria, useful for Reconquest. About half the cores disappear in 1495.

tags/tirol.html.title: Tirol
tags/tirol.html.description: Hidden tag in Austria, useful for Reconquest. Has a Gold Mine.

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
